steps.title: Identify the pretraining-baselines critique Text 2 targets
steps.content: Text 2 lists four methodological flaws in entrepreneurial-training research. The flaw relevant to the correct answer is the failure to establish pretraining baselines for the measured attributes of participants — that is, studies never record where participants stood on key attributes before training began, making it impossible to judge how much training actually changed them.

steps.title: Match this concern to what Text 1 leaves unanswered
steps.content:
To evaluate whether Cruz's study has that flaw, the Text 2 author needs to know the timing of the measurements. Choice A asks whether Cruz and colleagues measured attributes after the training — exactly the timing question that matters. If attributes were only measured post-training, there is no pretraining baseline, and Text 2's critique applies directly.

Text 1 reports outcomes but never specifies when the attribute measurements were taken, so this question remains genuinely open.

  • B — asks about sample size; Text 2 does not flag sample size as the relevant gap.
  • C — asks about participant characteristics at selection; that addresses a different methodological concern, not the baseline timing issue.
  • D — asks about comparison to existing entrepreneurs; Text 2's baseline critique is about within-study timing, not an external comparison group.

That confirms A is correct.
